title: SSE parse helpers should use typed structs not manual field extraction
description: parse_typing_data and parse_presence_data both do the same .get().and_then(|x| x.as_str()) dance for each field with an early-return-on-None for required fields. This is the shape of typed-struct deserialization done by hand, which is worse than the real thing: it is verbose, easy to get wrong on new fields, and does not document the shape of the expected JSON. Rewrite: define private #[derive(Deserialize)] structs TypingPayload and PresencePayload with the exact fields, mark optional ones as Option<String>, and replace the manual extraction with serde_json::from_str::<TypingPayload>(data). This also eliminates the asymmetry where parse_state_data goes through serde_json::from_value for its inner map while the other two functions do manual extraction.
